stemHtml: After commencing the descent, the pilot of a jet aircraft is instructed by the ATC to keep the speed high and issues an altitude restriction up to FL120 at a waypoint 50 NM before the planned position. The pilot realizes that he/she will not be able to meet the restriction with the current rate of descent. What action(s) should he/she do?

options.a: Decrease the rate of descent while increasing IAS to below V<sub>MO</sub> and keep this speed until reaching FL120. If needed, deploy speed brakes to remain on the descent profile.
options.b: Increase the rate of descent while increasing MACH speed to below M<sub>MO </sub>and keep this speed until reaching FL120. If needed, deploy speed brakes to remain on the descent profile.
options.c: Increase the rate of descent while increasing MACH speed to below M<sub>MO</sub> until the crossover altitude, after which continues with an IAS below Vmo. If needed, deploy speed brakes to remain on the descent profile.
options.d: Decrease the rate of descent and keep the calculated descent speed. If needed, deploy speed brakes to remain on the descent profile.
